About Me: Abdul Abiad is Deputy Chief Economist at the Asian Development Bank in Manila. He provides strategic guidance for ADB’s economic research and flagship reports. From 2000 to 2015, he was with the International Monetary Fund in Washington DC, where he worked on the World Economic Outlook. His research has focused on financial sector development, international financial integration, fiscal policy, monetary policy, exchange rates, trade, economic resilience, and infrastructure. A Philippine national, he has a BS in Mathematics from the University of the Philippines and a Ph.D. in Economics from the University of Pennsylvania

Division: Office of the Chief Economist and Director General
Expertise List: Development economics; Labor and demographic economics; Health, education, and welfare
About Me:
Albert F. Park is Chief Economist of the Asian Development Bank (ADB) and Director General of its Economic Research and Development Impact Department. He is chief spokesperson on economic and development trends and leads the production of ADB’s flagship knowledge products and support for regional cooperation fora.
Mr. Park has more than 2 decades of experience as a development economist and is a well-known expert on the economy of the People’s Republic of China. He has worked on a broad range of development issues including poverty and inequality, intergenerational mobility, microfinance, migration and labor markets, the future of work, and foreign investment.
Mr. Park is Chair Professor of Economics, Social Science, and Public Policy at HKUST (on leave). He served as a founding director of HKUST’s Institute for Emerging Market Studies and Center for Economic Policy, and previously held faculty positions at the University of Oxford and University of Michigan. A national of the United States, he received his bachelor’s degree in economics from Harvard University and his doctorate in applied economics from Stanford University.

About Me: Art Martinez, Jr. is a Senior Statistician at the Asian Development Bank, where he supports the Key Indicators for Asia and the Pacific, ADB’s main statistical publication. His research and publications focus on poverty measurement, social mobility, inequality of opportunity, SDG indicators, with a strong emphasis on innovative methods such as big data, satellite imagery, and geospatial analytics to improve statistical granularity and policy relevance in developing member countries.ember countries. Before joining ADB, Art was a Research Fellow at the University of Queensland’s Institute for Social Science Research, studying social and economic inequality and mobility using large-scale survey and administrative data. He holds a PhD in Social Statistics from the University of Queensland and an MS in Statistics from the University of the Philippines. He has published on multidimensional poverty, social mobility, inequality, application of big data, and survey sampling design.

About Me: Daniel conducts applied economics research in human development, poverty, and social policy in low- and middle-income countries. He has published nearly 40 research articles in economics journals, including the American Economic Journal: Applied Economics and Journal of Development Economics. Daniel is a co-editor of Economics of Education Review and an associate editor of Asian Development Review. He has advised the Indonesian Ministries of Economic Affairs, Education, Finance, and Health. He is involved in research partnerships with the governments of the Philippines, Bhutan, and Cambodia. Daniel has a doctorate in economics from the Australian National University.

Expertise List: Macroeconomics and monetary economics; Quantitative analysis; Statistics
About Me: Jaqueson Galimberti is a Senior Economist at the Economic Research and Development Impact Department at the Asian Development Bank. Prior to joining ADB, Jaqueson worked as a Senior Economic Analyst at the Reserve Bank of New Zealand and as an Associate Professor at Auckland University of Technology. He obtained his PhD in Economics from the University of Manchester and worked as a postdoctoral researcher at ETH Zurich. His research is about macroeconomics with a focus on modeling, measurement, and forecasting. He has a track record of publications in peer-reviewed academic journals in macroeconomics and computational economics.

Expertise List: Statistics; Development economics
About Me: Jeffrey B. Napoles is an Associate Statistics Officer at the Asian Development Bank (ADB), where he develops and maintains statistical database systems, builds internal automation tools, and contributes to flagship data publications. Prior to his current role, he served as a Consultant at ADB — leading cloud migration of enterprise databases, designing data visualization dashboards, and supporting international data standardization initiatives across the Asia-Pacific region.

Expertise List: International economics; Macroeconomics and monetary economics; Financial economics
About Me: John Beirne is a Principal Economist at the Asian Development Bank (ADB) in the Philippines. Previously, he was Vice-Chair of Research and a Senior Fellow at the ADB Institute in Japan, and before that, a Senior Economist at the European Central Bank in Germany. Earlier in his career, he worked in academia and the private sector in the United Kingdom and Ireland. John’s research and policy work focuses on exchange rates, commodities, financial crisis and contagion, fiscal policy, and monetary policy transmission. He has published widely in journals and holds a PhD in Economics from Brunel University of London.

Expertise List: International economics; Macroeconomics and monetary economics; Environmental and ecological economics
About Me: Jong Woo Kang is Director of Regional Cooperation and Integration Division. As director, he plays a role as an interface between ADB’s internal and external stakeholders on regional cooperation and integration. He was Principal Economist at ERDI, Senior Advisor to the Managing Director General of ADB and Senior Economist at Strategy and Policy Department. He leads the annual publication of Asian Economic Integration Report. Areas of his research interest include regional integration, inclusive growth, macroeconomic policies, international trade and finance, and aid effectiveness. Before joining ADB, he was director at Ministry of Finance and Economy of Korea until 2006.

Expertise List: Financial economics
About Me: Kosintr (Pok) Puongsophol is a Senior Financial Sector Specialist at the Asian Development Bank, specializing in local currency bond market development and sustainable finance across ASEAN+3. He leads ADB’s Green, Social, Sustainable, and Other Labeled (GSS+) Bonds Initiative, a strategic initiative under the ASEAN+3 Asian Bond Markets Initiative and the ASEAN Catalytic Green Finance Facility, with a strong focus on catalyzing innovative GSS+ transactions and strengthening the sustainable finance ecosystem in Southeast Asia. He has worked with over 20 issuers to mobilize more than $15 billion in local currency sustainable finance transactions.

Expertise List: Statistics; Quantitative analysis; Development economics
About Me: Experienced professional dedicated to international development through evidence based policy making at the global and local levels, with portfolio of projects covering several ADB member countries. Collaborates effectively with national governments and international organizations to develop statistical and analytical capacities in countries in Asia and the Pacific. Equips and enables policy makers through high quality data to study their respective economies and their interconnections to global production networks. Leads multi-country, multi-functional teams in modernizing statistical infrastructures. Facilitates the use of advanced technologies and innovative data in official statistics. Specialized in transforming diverse and multi sourced data into actionable economic information.

About Me: Dr Matteo Lanzafame is Director of the Macroeconomics Research Division, in the Economic Research and Development Impact Department of the Asian Development Bank (ADB). He is a macroeconomist and conducts research in many areas, with a particular focus on growth and development issues. He leads the team that produces ADB's Asian Development Outlook and conducts macroeconomic surveillance and monitoring. Prior to joining ADB in 2019, Matteo was Associate Professor of Political Economy at the University of Messina, in Italy. He holds an MA in Development Economics and a PhD in Economics from the University of Kent, in the UK.

Expertise List: Development economics; International economics; Quantitative analysis
About Me: I am an economist specializing in international trade, global value chains, innovation, structural change, and economic development. With more than 25 years of experience across academia and international organizations, I am currently Principal Economist at the Asian Development Bank. My research focuses on the intersection of trade, technology, structural tranformation, and sustainable development, with extensive work on productivity, technological upgrading, and global production networks. I have published widely in economics journals and led major policy reports for institutions including the Asian Development Bank, UNIDO, UN-ECLAC, and UNU-MERIT, contributing evidence-based analysis to development and trade policy debates globally.

About Me: Patrick Jaime A. Simba is an Economics Analyst in the Macroeconomic Research (ERMR) division of the Asian Development Bank. His work focuses on macroeconomic monitoring, applied economic research, and statistical analysis, with contributions to flagship publications such as the Asian Development Outlook and country studies. He has a strong background in data science, specializing in machine learning engineering and natural language processing (NLP) models, and supports analytical work through large‑scale data management, automation, and the development of data‑driven tools and dashboards.
Education: Bachelor of Science in Quantitative Economics and Econometrics (Minor in Management), University of Minnesota – Twin Cities, College of Liberal Arts, Minneapolis, Minnesota, United States

Expertise List: Public economics; Labor and demographic economics; Health, education, and welfare
About Me: Rhea Molato-Gayares is an Economics Officer at the Economic Research and Development Impact Department of ADB. She conducts policy-oriented research on labor, education, poverty, and social protection. She works with governments in formulating and implementing policy research. She holds a Ph.D. in Public Economics from the Ludwig-Maximilians-University of Munich and an M.A. in Economics from the University of the Philippines. She has published journal articles, working papers, policy reports, and policy briefs on various topics in development economics. Her work has been cited by media and served as input into ADB’s operations and governments’ policies.

Expertise List: Development economics; Macroeconomics and monetary economics; Quantitative analysis
About Me: My work examines the economic implications of development challenges and related policies. I use economic models to devise policy options that address these development challenges. Before joining ADB, I worked as a consultant with the World Bank, where I co-developed economic models to assess the impact pathways of climate transition policies to inform advisory and analytical products. I obtained a PhD in economics from the Australian National University, with research focused on the economic effects of COVID-19, climate change, and antimicrobial resistance.

About Me: Shigehiro is a Senior Economist at the Economic Research and Development Impact Department (ERDI) of the Asian Development Bank (ADB). He supports ADB’s developing member countries in designing small and medium-sized enterprise (SME) development policies through various technical assistance projects. His research expertise includes SME development and inclusive finance in developing Asia. Prior to joining ADB, he held several expert positions at Japan’s Ministry of Finance, Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) in France, and as Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA) expert on capital market development in Indonesia. He holds PhD in International Studies from Waseda University in Japan.

Expertise List: Quantitative analysis; Statistics; Development economics; Data science
About Me: Takaaki Masaki is a Senior Economist (Data Science) in the Data Division of the Economic Research and Development Impact Department at ADB. His work focuses on leveraging GIS, big data, remote sensing, and machine learning to strengthen development statistics—particularly agricultural and rural statistics—and to advance high-resolution welfare and poverty mapping. Through these approaches, he supports spatial targeting to maximize development impact and evaluates the distributional implications of policy interventions. Prior to joining ADB, he worked as a Senior Economist at the World Bank, where he led a number of analytical projects using innovative data sources—such as satellite and remote-sensing data—to improve welfare and poverty measurement, enhance agricultural statistics, assess spatial inequalities, and inform evidence-based policy design in data-scarce environments.

Expertise List: International economics; Development economics; Quantitative analysis
About Me: Wenyin Cheng is an Economist of the Economic Research and Development Impact department. He specializes in global value chains, regional cooperation and integration, and innovation. His career spaned academia (Tsinghua University and IDE-JETRO), think tanks (Institute for Contemporary China Studies), and international organizations (OECD). His research has been published in peer-reviewed journals, including Technovation, Technological Forecasting and Social Change, China Economic Review, Industrial and Corporate Change, etc. Additionally, he serves as an editorial board member for a few journals. He holds a Ph.D. from Tsinghua University, including one year in a joint PhD program at Columbia University.

Division: Economic Analysis and Operational Support Division
Expertise List: Environmental and ecological economics; Infrastructure sectors
About Me: Yi Jiang is an economist and Director in the Economic Analysis and Operational Support Division of the Asian Development Bank’s Economic Research and Development Impact Department. His current research focuses on urban infrastructure and its broader economic impacts, as well as urban development and climate change. His work has been published in journals such as Economic Development and Cultural Change, Journal of Development Economics, World Bank Economic Review, and World Development. He previously worked in ADB’s East Asia Department from 2011 to 2015 on lending and policy advisory projects in the agriculture, natural resources, and environment sectors.
